name: Skills Catalog
description: The 271 bundled skill specs (SKILL.md) and their supporting assets — parser scripts, templates, and per-skill JSON/config — that define every reusable capability ECC exposes to Claude Code agents.

name: Agents & Commands
description: Spec-driven Markdown definitions for all 67 named agents, 92 slash commands, 3 context presets, and 13 backward-compatibility command shims that form ECC's agentic dispatch surface.

name: Coding Rules
description: Per-language and cross-cutting Markdown coding-standard rules (Python, Rust, TypeScript, React, etc.) that ECC injects into agent sessions to enforce consistent development practices.

name: Automation Scripts
description: Node.js operational backbone — the install pipeline (lib/), lifecycle hook dispatchers (hooks/), CI helpers (ci/), Codex tooling, Discord bots, and the hook configuration manifests that wire ECC's event-driven behaviors.

name: Rust Control Plane
description: The ecc2 Rust binary that provides a TUI dashboard, session management daemon, inter-process communication, observability, and git worktree control — the high-performance native layer of the ECC stack.

name: LLM & Integration Layer
description: Python provider-agnostic LLM abstraction (claude, openai, ollama, astraflow) with prompt builder and CLI selector, plus the Aura integration adapter — the AI inference plumbing underlying ECC's intelligence features.

name: Test Suite
description: Jest and shell-based test suite covering lifecycle hooks, install library functions, CI scripts, and integration scenarios — the primary quality gate for ECC's Node.js automation layer.

name: Plugin Manifest & Configuration
description: The Claude Code plugin manifests (plugin.json, marketplace.json), JSON schemas for install components and profiles, MCP server configs, Cursor scaffolds, evaluator-rag prototype fixtures, example CLAUDE.md templates, and all root-level configuration files that define and govern ECC's deployable structure.

name: CI/CD & Project Documentation
description: GitHub Actions CI/CD pipelines (release, test, SLSA provenance, supply-chain watch), GitHub project config (dependabot, funding, PR templates, Copilot prompts), and all root-level Markdown documentation (README, CHANGELOG, CONTRIBUTING, guides, SOUL) plus the research archive.
